channel.title: Naija Digest Online
channel.description: Nigeria’s one-stop hub for breaking news, tech, football, celebrity gossip, Naija music, live TV, exchange rates and trending topics.

item.title: Adeleke demands visa ban for APC chieftains, party faults gov
item.dc:creator: Punch
item.description:
Osun Governor Ademola Adeleke seeks economic sanctions and a visa ban on APC chieftains, citing political violence ahead of the 2026 elections.
Read More: https://punchng.com/adeleke-demands-visa-ban-for-apc-chieftains-party-faults-gov/

item.title: Lagos begins recruitment of doctors, nurses, others
item.dc:creator: Punch
item.description:
Lagos State Government opens recruitment for doctors, nurses, and other clinical staff across 29 healthcare facilities. Apply now to join Lagos medical rec
Read More: https://punchng.com/lagos-begins-recruitment-of-doctors-nurses-others/

item.title: Anambra agency cracks down on illegal street trading, shanties in markets
item.dc:creator: Punch
item.description:
OCHA Brigade in Anambra intensifies crackdown on illegal street trading and shanties in markets across Ogidi, Nkpor, and other areas to ensure public safet
Read More: https://punchng.com/anambra-agency-cracks-down-on-illegal-street-trading-shanties-in-markets/

item.title: If anyone can beat France, it’s Spain — Yamal
item.dc:creator: Punch
item.description:
Lamine Yamal believes Spain can overcome France in their highly anticipated FIFA World Cup semi-final clash. Read more about Spain’s confidence.
Read More: https://punchng.com/if-anyone-can-beat-france-its-spain-yamal/

item.title: Oluremi Tinubu assures World Bank of robust school feeding programme
item.dc:creator: Punch
item.description:
First Lady Oluremi Tinubu assures the World Bank of the Federal Government’s commitment to establishing a robust school feeding programme in Nigeria.
Read More: https://punchng.com/oluremi-tinubu-assures-world-bank-of-robust-school-feeding-programme/

item.title: Abducted Oyo farmer regains freedom
item.dc:creator: Punch
item.description:
A 60-year-old Oyo farmer, Mathew Owoade, abducted in Itesiwaju LGA, has regained freedom thanks to a coordinated police rescue operation. Read more.
Read More: https://punchng.com/abducted-oyo-farmer-regains-freedom/

item.title: NCDC condemns Benue attacks, demands swift justice for perpetrators
item.dc:creator: Daily Post
item.description: The North Central Development Commission (NCDC) has expressed profound grief and concern over the reported attacks on the Otukpo-Nobi and Akpachi-Ugboju communities in Otukpo Local Government Area of Benue State. The attacks resulted in the tragic loss of lives and the destruction of homes and other

item.title: JUST IN: Tinubu Appoints Kola-Oyeneyin, Mutallab As NIPC, NEPZA Board Chairpersons
item.dc:creator: TVC News
item.description: President Bola Ahmed Tinubu has approved the appointment of Eyitope Kola-Oyeneyin and Muhammad Hadi Mutallab as board chairpersons of the Nigerian Investment Promotion Commission (NIPC) and Nigerian Export Processing Zones Authority (NEPZA), respectively. The Federal Ministry of Industry, Trade and

item.title: OnePlus is reportedly bailing on the US
item.dc:creator: The Verge
item.description: OnePlus and its parent company, Oppo, plan to announce in the coming days that OnePlus brand will be leaving the US and European markets, according to a machine translation of a WinFuture report. Should the exit actually happen, it will mark a conclusion to months of rumors about the future of OnePl

item.title: Wells Fargo’s Mike Mayo: Citi My Top Pick of Big Banks
item.dc:creator: Bloomberg Markets
item.description: Investors are preparing for a significant earnings week as five of the six largest US banks, including JPMorgan, Citi, Wells Fargo, Bank of America, and Goldman Sachs, are scheduled to report their quarterly results within a few hours of each other. Mike Mayo, Head of US Large-Cap Bank Research at W

item.title: Sokoto Govt Launches Free School Bus Service To Boost Access To Education
item.dc:creator: New Telegraph
item.description: The Sokoto State Government has approved free transportation for all students using government-operated school buses across the Sokoto metropolis and surrounding communities, effective Monday, July 13, 2026. The State Commissioner for Basic and Secondary Education, Professor Ahmad Ladan Ala, gave th

item.title: Trump reinstates Iran port blockade and vows 20% charge on cargo passing through Hormuz
item.dc:creator: BBC News World
item.description: The US president also says Washington will impose a 20% charge on all cargo shipped through the Strait of Hormuz.

item.title: Okpebholo Grants N41m Soft Loans To Edo Returnees Evacuated From S’Africa
item.dc:creator: New Telegraph
item.description: Edo State Governor, Senator Monday Okpebholo, has approved a total of N41 million as immediate financial support for 41 Edo indigenes evacuated from South Africa following xenophobic attacks, with each returnee to receive N1 million to help them secure accommodation and begin rebuilding their lives.

item.title: Rescued Oyo School Principal Recounts Ordeal
item.dc:creator: ThisDay
item.description: Kemi Olaitan in Ibadan The Principal of the school, Mrs..Rachael Alamu, has recounted the ordeal they endured, describing the experience as traumatic and emotionally draining. Alamu while speaking with journalists

item.title: ‘VAR-gentina?’Conspiracy Theories Swirl Ahead Of World Cup Semi-Finals
item.dc:creator: Channels TV
item.description: Social media is awash with AI-generated videos and memes which amplify this narrative, whether it's FIFA President Gianni Infantino and Messi sharing a loving embrace on the deck of the Titanic in the style of Leonardo DiCaprio and Kate Winslet or simply Infantino's face being superimposed at the he

item.title: 💦 Why hydration breaks could be here to stay
item.dc:creator: ESPN Soccer
item.description: FIFA's mandated three-minute breaks have been a talking point throughout the 2026 World Cup.

item.title: NPFL: John Joshua set for Nasarawa United exit
item.dc:creator: Daily Post
item.description: John Joshua is on the verge of completing a move away from Nigeria Premier Football League, NPFL, side Nasarawa United. Joshua and the Solid Miners are yet to agreement on a new contract. The midfielder played a crucial role in the club’s fifth place finish in the NPFL last season. Nasarawa Un

item.title: Troops Arrest Terrorists’ Logistics Suppliers, Recover Weapons In Kaduna
item.dc:creator: Leadership
item.description: Troops of Sector 7 Operation ENDURING PEACE (OPEP) during stop and search operations at Samaru Village, in Zonkwa district of Zangon Kataf Local Government Area of Kaduna State have successfully intercepted terrorists logistics suppliers, recovered weapons and arrested suspects who were traveling f

item.title: EU expands Gaza support, slams IOC invite to Russian athletes
item.dc:creator: Daily Post
item.description: The European Union has restated its solidarity with Gazans, describing the alliance as the Palestinian people’s “most credible supporter” and “strongest backer of the two-state solution.” On Monday, High Representative Kaja Kallas reaffirmed that all 27 Member States ag

item.title: Fintiri’s Secret to Successful Governance & Responsible Resource Management
item.dc:creator: Daily Trust
item.description: One of the defining secrets behind Governor Ahmadu Umaru Fintiri’s success in governance is his approach to resource management; particularly in the distribution, application, and generation of public resources. Since assuming office in 2019, the Fintiri administration has recorded remarkable interv

item.title: Nigerian hospital celebrates survival of its smallest preterm baby
item.dc:creator: Premium Times
item.description: A baby girl born at 25 weeks and weighing just 750g has been discharged from the hospital after six months of intensive care, marking the facility's smallest surviving newborn and underscoring its growing capacity to manage extremely premature infants. The post Nigerian hospital celebrates survival

item.title: Adamawa Government Begins Construction of 3.7km Road in Hildi
item.dc:creator: Daily Trust
item.description: The Adamawa State Government has commenced the construction of a 3.7-kilometre road in Hildi Town, reaffirming Governor Ahmadu Umaru Fintiri’s commitment to expanding infrastructure and improving the quality of life across communities in the state. Work has already commenced on the project, with the

item.title: Tinubu’s wife receives World Bank team
item.dc:creator: Tribune
item.description: First Lady of Nigeria, Senator Oluremi Tinubu, has reaffirmed the present administration’s commitment to investment in early childhood development. The First Lady made the declaration on Monday during an engagement with a delegation from the World Bank at the State House ,Abuja. The five-man d

item.title: Lindsey Graham's sister chosen as replacement after senator's death
item.dc:creator: BBC News World
item.description: The South Carolina governor chose the senator's little sister to serve out the rest of his term after Graham's death on Saturday.

item.title: Abia jails two sexual offenders, opens register to name and shame convicts
item.dc:creator: Daily Post
item.description: The Abia State Government has said it has successfully prosecuted two sexual offenders in the state for defiling two minors, stating that the suspects have been convicted and jailed by a Family Court sitting at the Umuahia High Court. The state’s Commissioner for Information, Okey Kanu, disclo

item.title: World Cup 2026: We’re Not Afraid Of Spain –Rabiot
item.dc:creator: Completesports
item.description: AC Milan star Adrien Rabiot has disclosed that the French national team does not fear Spain ahead of their FIFA World Cup 2026 semi-final clash. He emphasized that France does not need a special anti-Yamal plan and is solely focused on playing a solid, collective match against a highly dangerous Spa

item.title: Police rescue kidnapped farmer in Oyo
item.dc:creator: Daily Post
item.description: The Oyo State Police Command has announced the rescue of a victim of a recent kidnapping in the state. The command said Mr Owoade Matthew, who was kidnapped on July 13, has been rescued. The announcement was made in a statement signed by the command’s Public Relations Officer, Olayinka Ay

item.title: Man bags 20-Year Jail Term for Shielding Bandit
item.dc:creator: Daily Trust
item.description: A Federal High Court sitting in Abuja, on Monday, sentenced one Muhammad Sani to 20 years imprisonment for conspiring to conceal information about an alleged notorious bandit, Danranmi, which led to his escape. Sani of Nabi Village in Shiroro Local Government Area of Niger State, was convicted on Co

item.title: FCT Natives Hail Youth Network For Strengthening Advocacy, Leadership Development
item.dc:creator: Leadership
item.description: Natives of the Federal Capital Territory (FCT) under the auspices of Abuja Original Inhabitants Youths Empowerment Organisation (AOIYEO) said the establishment of FCT Youth Network (FCT-YN) has brought greater coordination to indigenous youth advocacy, promoted peaceful engagement with government an

item.title: Lagos begins Hajj form sales, sets ₦7m deposit for pilgrims
item.dc:creator: Punch
item.description:
Lagos State Muslim Pilgrims Welfare Board opens Hajj form sales for 2027, requiring a ₦7m deposit. Register early for a seamless pilgrimage experience.
Read More: https://punchng.com/lagos-begins-hajj-form-sales-sets-%e2%82%a67m-deposit-for-pilgrims/

item.title: How Lagos emergency responders saved my baby during midnight downpour — Resident
item.dc:creator: Tribune
item.description: A Badagry resident, Muhammad Armayau, has commended the Lagos State emergency response team for its swift intervention in The post How Lagos emergency responders saved my baby during midnight downpour — Resident appeared first on Tribune Online.

item.title: Dangote Refinery Resumes Sale Of Petrol In Dollars
item.dc:creator: Daily Trust
item.description: Dangote Petroleum Refinery has switched payment for the sale of Premium Motor Spirit (PMS), Automotive Gas Oil (AGO), and Aviation Turbine Kerosene (ATK) to U.S. dollar. The new policy, which took effect immediately, applies to both gantry and coastal product sales. Industry sources familiar with th

item.title: Kidnapped Oyo headmaster regains freedom after 48 hours
item.dc:creator: Tribune
item.description: A middle-aged headmaster named Mr Owoade Mathew has regained his freedom after being kidnapped on his farm and held captive for 48 hours. Mr Owoade was abducted on his farmland located on the Igbojaye-Budo Aare farm road in the Itesiwaju local government area of Oyo State at 9:50 AM on Saturday. The

item.title: Oyebanji Retains Governing Councils Of State-Owned Tertiary Institutions
item.dc:creator: New Telegraph
item.description: Ekiti State Governor, Biodun Oyebanji, has approved the retention of the governing councils of all state-owned tertiary institutions for another term, citing their outstanding performance in repositioning higher education in the state. The Governor said the decision is aimed at sustaining stability,

item.title: England to make late call on Rice for World Cup semi-final
item.dc:creator: BBC Sport Football
item.description: England will make a late decision on whether Declan Rice is fit to start Wednesday's World Cup semi-final against Argentina.

item.title: Just in: US military begins third night of strikes on Iran
item.dc:creator: Vanguard
item.description: The US military said Monday it had launched a fresh salvo of attacks on Iran, marking the third night in a row that it had struck the country, as a deal to end the war falters. The post Just in: US military begins third night of strikes on Iran appeared first on Vanguard News.

item.title: Experts raise alarm over threats to water quality in Niger
item.dc:creator: Daily Post
item.description: Experts have expressed concern over the increasing threats posed by pollution, climate change and other human activities to water resources in Niger State, calling for urgent action to protect water bodies from further degradation. The warning was issued during a sensitisation programme on the prote

item.title: ‘I’m Not Hiding, I Only Fear for My Life,’ Says Adeyemi Amid ‘Presidential Council’ Controversy
item.dc:creator: Channels TV
item.description: Adeyemi, however, said he was prepared to defend himself in court. The post ‘I’m Not Hiding, I Only Fear for My Life,’ Says Adeyemi Amid ‘Presidential Council’ Controversy appeared first on Channels Television.

item.title: Championship: Arokodare omitted from Wolves’ Portugal training camp
item.dc:creator: Daily Post
item.description: Tolu Arokodare has been left out of Wolves’ squad for their pre-season tour of Portugal amid the uncertainty surrounding his future at the club. Arokodare is keen to leave the Molineux this summer following the club’s relegation last season. Wolves rejected a loan offer from Serie A club

item.title: Police in South Africa open probe into post-World Cup death of Jayden Adams
item.dc:creator: Al Jazeera
item.description: The country's football players' union confirmed Adams's death on Saturday, but the cause has not been revealed.

item.title: One year after death, Buhari’s life still ‘speaks’ to Nigerians – Tinubu
item.dc:creator: Premium Times
item.description: President Tinubu says Mr Buhari’s transformation from military ruler to civilian democrat marked him out as a rare figure in the nation's history. The post One year after death, Buhari’s life still ‘speaks’ to Nigerians – Tinubu appeared first on Premium Times Nigeria.

item.title: Police Arrest Man Over Alleged Murder Of 20-Year-Old Wife In Nasarawa
item.dc:creator: Leadership
item.description: The Nasarawa State Police Command has arrested a 30-year -old man in connection with the death of his wife, 20, in Agyaragu, Obi Local Government Area, following an alleged domestic dispute. The suspect, identified as Musbawu Amodu, was taken into custody on Sunday, July 12, 2026, after a complaint

item.title: Late US senator’s sister appointed to fill vacant seat
item.dc:creator: Vanguard
item.description: Darline Graham Nordone will serve in the US Senate until Graham's term expires in January, following his unexpected death at the weekend at the age of 71. The post Late US senator’s sister appointed to fill vacant seat appeared first on Vanguard News.

item.title: Illegal mining, money laundering fueling insecurity- Reps
item.dc:creator: The Sun
item.description: From Ndubuisi Orji, Abuja The House of Representatives has identified illegal mining and money laundering as major drivers of insecurity in the country. The chairman, House Ad Hoc Committee on Mineral Exploitation, Security and Anti-Money Laundering, Sanni Abdulraheem, stated this, on Monday at a

item.title: World Cup semi-finals: 8 Arsenal players eying glory [See names]
item.dc:creator: Daily Post
item.description: At least eight Arsenal players will be playing at the World Cup semi-final stage this week. France will tackle Spain in the first semi-final tie on Tuesday, while Argentina will take on England in the second semi-final fixture on Wednesday. The winners of both fixtures will qualify for the

item.title: Brazil court bars Bolsonaro son from visiting father until after election
item.dc:creator: Vanguard
item.description: Brazil's top court on Monday barred presidential hopeful Flavio Bolsonaro from visiting his father, the former president Jair Bolsonaro, who is under house arrest, until after the first round of October elections. The post Brazil court bars Bolsonaro son from visiting father until after election app

item.title: Remembrance: Buhari remains an epitome of integrity, modesty in life and death — Buratai
item.dc:creator: Tribune
item.description: Former Chief of Army Staff, Lt. Gen. Tukur Yusuf Buratai (rtd), has stated that former President Muhammadu Buhari epitomizes integrity and modesty in both life and death. In a statement issued on Monday to commemorate the one-year anniversary of President Buhari’s passing, Buratai, who previou

item.title: Kano court upholds journalists’ right to protect sources
item.dc:creator: Daily Post
item.description: A Federal High Court sitting in Kano has upheld the right of journalists to refuse to disclose their sources of information, except in matters involving a breach of national security or where the information has a direct link to a case before the court. The court also affirmed its power to subpoena

item.title: Haaland, teammates receive heroes’ welcome in Norway after World Cup exit
item.dc:creator: Vanguard
item.description: Erling Haaland's side were knocked out of the tournament on Saturday after losing 2-1 to England in extra-time in the last eight in Miami, their best result in the competition. item.title: Residents find abandoned baby boy in Kwara
item.dc:creator: Punch
item.description:
Residents of Omupo, Kwara State, found an abandoned baby boy with his placenta still attached. The newborn is now stable and receiving medical care.
Read More: https://punchng.com/residents-find-abandoned-baby-boy-in-kwara/

item.dc:creator: New Telegraph
item.description: The Principal of the secondary school abducted alongside teachers and pupils in Oriire Local Government Area of Oyo State, Mrs Racheal Alamu, has opened up on their gruesome experience during captivity. According to her, during their 56-day ordeal in captivity, children were beaten into silence, mal

The APM National Publicity Secretary, Abubakar Yusuf, stated this,on Monday,in Abuja. He said this is as result of Mak

item.description: Russian billionaire Dmitry Rybolovlev’s family trust prevailed in its effort to remove the manager of a bankrupt $2.5 billion venture capital fund it backed.

item.title: Argentina IPO Pipeline Grows as YPF Power Unit Files For US Listing
item.dc:creator: Bloomberg Markets
item.description: The power-generation unit of Argentina’s state-run energy firm YPF SA has filed for a US initial public offering, joining a growing pipeline of Argentine companies seeking New York listings.

item.description: Late US senator backed invasion of Iraq, war on Iran and approach of 'more for Israel' as he became a Trump confidant.

item.description: Strategic Petroleum Reserve stockpiles are precariously low and have been hit by major equipment failures, leaks and spills, according to a government report.

item.title: Satya Nadella has issued a shocking warning to companies using AI
item.dc:creator: TechCrunch
item.description: Of all the debates raging about the potential downsides of AI, there is one worry causing the most hand-wringing among AI enthusiasts in Silicon Valley — that the giant AI labs that sell proprietary models are somehow acting like Trojan horses.

item.title: The Shokz OpenRun Pro are the cheapest they’ve been since January
item.dc:creator: The Verge
item.description: Noise-canceling earbuds are great for flights and focusing, but they're not always ideal for outdoor workouts. The last-gen Shokz OpenRun Pro's open-ear design lets you enjoy music while staying aware of approaching cars, cyclists, and other potential hazards, and right now they're on sale for $109

item.title: Foreign investment: Kalu calls for Nigeria-EU Communication Strategy
item.dc:creator: The Sun
item.description: From Ndubuisi Orji, Abuja The Deputy Speaker of the House of Representatives, Benjamin Kalu, has called for deliberate “co-communication” between Nigeria and the European Union, as well as the adoption of near-shoring models, so as to change negative global narratives and boost investor confidence i

item.title: Andy Burnham in line to become British PM after securing party support
item.dc:creator: Al Jazeera
item.description: Burnham's bid cannot be challenged by fellow MPs, as he now has the support of almost the entire parliamentary party.

item.title: Oriire rescue: Alaafin visits victims, donates cash
item.dc:creator: Tribune
item.description: The Alaafin of Oyo, Oba Akeem Owoade, had on Monday visited the teachers and pupils rescued from captivity following The post Oriire rescue: Alaafin visits victims, donates cash appeared first on Tribune Online.

item.title: The Pixel colors might rule this year
item.dc:creator: The Verge
item.description: This year's Google Pixel 11 lineup might come in a bunch of funky colors. A series of now-deleted Amazon listings spotted by 9to5Google show what appear to be placeholders for Google's upcoming Pixel 11 in hot pink Fuchsia (Hibiscus), vibrant green Moss (Pistachio), and Midnight (Obsidian) black. We

item.title: Parents Fear Returning Children To School After 56-Day Ordeal Of Rescued Oriire Victims
item.dc:creator: Leadership
item.description: Following the rescue of the abducted Oriire school teachers and students after 56 days in captivity, parents have expressed fear about returning their children back to classrooms. They argued that the traumatic experience of spending 56 days in captivity had left families unwilling to take further r

item.title: Argentina vs England World Cup tickets more expensive than France vs Spain
item.dc:creator: Al Jazeera
item.description: Prices for the Argentina vs England semifinal have seen a 34 percent increase over the past three days.

item.title: ETF IQ 7/13/2026
item.dc:creator: Bloomberg Markets
item.description: "Bloomberg ETF IQ" focuses on the opportunities, risks and current trends tied to the trillions of dollars in the global exchange traded funds industry. Today's guests: Former AQR Capital Management Head of Financial Market Research & Author of "Wrong Number" Aaron Brown, Capital Management CEO &

item.title: US strikes Iranian ship with drone boats in first-of-its-kind attack
item.dc:creator: Al Jazeera
item.description: US CENTCOM has published a video it says shows three Corsair unmanned surface vessels striking an Iranian ship facility

item.title: Lindsey Graham died from a rare heart condition. Here’s how to know if you’re at risk.
item.dc:creator: MarketWatch
item.description: Aortic dissections are extremely rare; however, people with a family history or who have been diagnosed with certain genetic syndromes have an elevated risk

item.title: I Borrowed N400m To Secure Appointment—Fake Agency DG
item.dc:creator: The Whistler
item.description: …Says Lenders Have Petitioned EFCC For Refund The embattled Director-General of the disowned Presidential Foreign Investment Promotion Council (PFIPC), Prince Adeniyi Adeyemi Matthew, has claimed that the N400m he allegedly paid to secure his appointment was borrowed and that his creditors have peti

item.title: Apple’s public betas for iOS 27 and more are out now
item.dc:creator: The Verge
item.description: Apple has just released public betas for iOS 27 and other major OS updates that are set to publicly launch this fall. The big new feature this year is Siri AI, the delayed AI-powered revamp to Siri. It actually works - which is big praise! - though it keeps things brief. Other betas available now [&

item.title: How troops rescued abducted Oyo pupils after 56 days — GOC
item.dc:creator: The Sun
item.description: The General Officer Commanding (GOC) of the Nigerian Army's 2 Division, Major General Chinedu Nnebeife, has revealed that the successful rescue of pupils, teachers and the principal kidnapped in Oriire Local Government Area of Oyo State followed a painstaking 56-day intelligence-led operation involv

item.title: When distribution weakened development, By Dipo Baruwa
item.dc:creator: Premium Times
item.description: Nigeria’s federal system presupposes an implicit alignment among the three tiers of government in the pursuit of coordinated growth and national development. Yet alignment is never automatic. In public policy, as in life, very little happens spontaneously. Every enduring system is built on rel

item.title: Siri AI makes the Apple Watch finally feel like a wrist computer
item.dc:creator: The Verge
item.description: Siri has been on the Apple Watch since day one, though I'm usually hard-pressed to find people who actually make good use of it. It's kind of just… been there - mostly as a way to set timers when my hands are full. But after playing around with the watchOS 27 developer beta, I get […]

item.title: UK awards £3.16m contracts to develop low-cost drone interceptors
item.dc:creator: Tribune
item.description: The Ministry of Defence has awarded £3.16 million to three suppliers to develop new low‑cost interceptors designed to shoot down drones and other airborne threats. The UK is the first of five European partner nations to award contracts under a joint programme to strengthen air defence. These interce
